violations.text: Handsink is used as a dump sink and for rinsing out coffee urns per PIC. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
The hand washing sink in the coffee area is lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them, with access to the 3 bay sink blocked by boxes. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Baked goods (yogurt muffins, donuts, bagels, cookies, brownies, scones, fritters, and Danish) packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. All products were relocated to behind the counter during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
The fan covers in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust and mold. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024
One market light panel was nonfunctional during inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on and beneath market shelves. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Terminex PCS monthly. Scheduled Pest Control Service must be increased to a minimum of weekly until rodent issue is resolved. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the market shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, rodent droppings, and other debris. The freezer inside the walk in cooler has an accumulation of frost and debris. The bagged ice freezer has a heavy accumulation of frost. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, plastic crates, and debris. Dumpster area must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the outside area near the shed. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024
The ceiling in the toilet room and market is in poor repair, with damaged and/or missing tiles. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2024
One market light panel was nonfunctional during inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024

The floors especially in the walk in cooler and beneath market shelves have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris and/or rodent droppings. The ceiling, walls, and fan covers in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and mold. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the market area near checkout. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024
